exercises.name: March in Place
exercises.instruction: Stand up and march with big steps, raising each thigh until it's perpendicular to your torso. 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raises
exercises.instruction: Stand up, rise onto your toes, lower back down. 20 reps. Arnold wouldn't let us skip calves.

exercises.name: Chair Squats
exercises.instruction: Sit down on your chair, then stand back up without using your hands. 10 reps.

exercises.name: Wall Pushups
exercises.instruction: Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height and width. Do pushups against the wall. 10 reps.

exercises.name: Desk Plank
exercises.instruction: Hands on your desk, body in a straight line from shoulders to ankles. Squeeze abs and glutes. Hold 20 seconds.

exercises.name: Bent Over Y's
exercises.instruction: Push hips back, torso parallel to floor. Arms hanging down, lift them up at 45 degrees to form a Y. 10 reps.

exercises.name: The Brettzel Stretch
exercises.instruction: Lie on your right side, left knee across body held by right hand, grab right foot with left hand. Rotate left shoulder toward ground. 4-5 breaths each side.

exercises.name: Cat Camel
exercises.instruction: On hands and knees, arch your back up like a cat (exhale), then drop your belly toward the floor (inhale). 5-8 slow reps.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.instruction: On hands and knees, extend your right arm forward and left leg back. Hold 2 seconds, return. Alternate. 5-8 reps each side.

exercises.name: Bent Over IYT Raises
exercises.instruction: Bend over, torso parallel to floor. Lift arms straight ahead (I), then at 45 degrees (Y), then out to sides (T). 5 reps each position.

exercises.name: Seated Twist
exercises.instruction: Sit tall in your chair, feet flat. Rotate your torso to the right, hold 5 seconds. Then left. 30 seconds total. Great for your spine.

exercises.name: Kneeling Thread-Through-to-Reach
exercises.instruction: On hands and knees, reach one arm under your body (threading the needle), then open up and reach to the sky. 3-5 reps each side.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squats
exercises.instruction: Feet shoulder-width apart. Squat down as low as comfortable, stand back up. 10 reps.

exercises.name: Desk Pushups
exercises.instruction: Hands on your desk edge, body straight. Lower your chest to the desk and push back up. 8 reps.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.instruction: Back flat against the wall, slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor. Hold for 20 seconds.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunges
exercises.instruction: Step one foot back, lower your knee toward the floor, return to standing. Alternate legs. 5 per side.
exercises.source: The Safest Way To Train To Failure

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.instruction: Lie on the floor, knees bent, feet flat. Push hips up squeezing your glutes. Hold 3 seconds at top. 10 reps.

exercises.name: Step-Ups
exercises.instruction: Find a sturdy step or low bench. Step up, step down, alternate legs. 10 reps per leg.

exercises.name: Standing Glute Squeeze
exercises.instruction: Stand with your back against the wall. Squeeze your glutes hard, pushing hips forward. Hold 10 seconds. Repeat 5 times.

exercises.name: Alternating Forward Lunges
exercises.instruction: Step forward, lower back knee toward floor, push back to standing. Alternate legs. 5 per side.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.instruction: Lie down, one foot flat on floor, other leg raised. Push hips up with the grounded leg. 8 reps per side.

exercises.name: Reverse Crunch
exercises.instruction: Lie on your back, knees bent. Curl your knees toward your chest, lifting hips off the floor. Controlled. 10 reps.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Raises
exercises.instruction: Lie flat on your back, legs straight. Raise both legs to 90 degrees, lower slowly. 10 reps. Great for lower abs.

exercises.name: Squat to Stand
exercises.instruction: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Bend down and grab your toes, drop your hips into a deep squat, then stand. 8 reps. Opens up the whole body.

exercises.name: Downward Dog to Spiderman Lunge
exercises.instruction: Start in downward dog. Step one foot to the outside of your hand into a deep lunge. Return and switch sides. 5 per side.

exercises.name: Slow Tempo Squats (5:3:1)
exercises.instruction: 5 seconds to lower, hold the bottom 3 seconds, 1 second to stand. Harder than they sound. Just 3 reps.

exercises.name: Slow Tempo Desk Pushups (5:3:1)
exercises.instruction: Hands on desk. 5 seconds down, hold at bottom 3 seconds, 1 second up. Just 3 reps.

exercises.name: 2-Minute Squat Challenge
exercises.instruction: Set a 2-minute timer. Do as many bodyweight squats as you can, resting when needed. Push yourself!

exercises.name: 2-Minute Desk Pushup Challenge
exercises.instruction: Set a 2-minute timer. Do as many desk pushups as you can, resting when needed. Push yourself!

exercises.name: Myo-Rep Squats
exercises.instruction: 15 bodyweight squats. Take 3-5 deep breaths. Do 3-5 more. Repeat the breathe-and-squat cycle 3 more times (5 sets total).

exercises.name: Myo-Rep Desk Pushups
exercises.instruction: 15 desk pushups. Take 3-5 deep breaths. Do 3-5 more. Repeat the breathe-and-push cycle 3 more times (5 sets total).

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.instruction: Hands on desk or floor, drive knees toward chest alternately. 20 seconds. Get that heart rate up!

exercises.name: Plank Walkout
exercises.instruction: Stand, bend forward, walk hands out to plank position, hold 2 seconds, walk hands back, stand. 5 reps.

exercises.name: Lateral Lunges
exercises.instruction: Feet wide. Shift weight to one side, bending that knee, other leg straight. Alternate. 5 per side.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.instruction: Lie on the floor, arms overhead, lift shoulders and legs off the ground. Hold 20 seconds. Great for your core.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Desk Pushups
exercises.instruction: Hands close together on your desk edge (thumbs almost touching). Lower chest to desk, push back up. Targets triceps. 6-10 reps.

exercises.name: Squat Jumps
exercises.instruction: Squat down, then explode upward jumping off the ground. Land softly, go straight into the next rep. 5-6 reps.

exercises.name: T-Pushups
exercises.instruction: Do a pushup (floor or desk). At the top, rotate and reach one arm to the sky forming a T. Alternate sides. 5 per side.

exercises.name: Isometric Pushup Hold
exercises.instruction: Lower into a desk pushup until arms are at 90 degrees. Hold that position. 20 seconds. Shake it out.

exercises.name: Isometric Squat Hold
exercises.instruction: Squat down to the bottom position and hold. No wall support. 20 seconds. Your quads will burn.

exercises.name: Isometric Lunge Hold
exercises.instruction: Step into a lunge and hold the bottom position. 15 seconds each leg. Breathe through it.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.instruction: Run in place, driving knees as high as possible. 15 seconds. Quick burst of cardio!

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.instruction: Hands and feet on the floor, knees hovering just off the ground. Crawl forward and back. 30 seconds. Works everything.

exercises.name: Bridge Hamstring Walkout
exercises.instruction: Lie down, push hips up into a bridge. Slowly walk your feet out until legs are nearly straight, then walk them back. 60 seconds.

exercises.name: Elevator Desk Pushups
exercises.instruction: Lower with 3 pauses on the way down (top, middle, bottom), then 3 pauses on the way up. Smooth and controlled. 5 reps.

exercises.name: 2-Minute Lunge Challenge
exercises.instruction: Set a 2-minute timer. Alternating lunges, as many as you can, resting when needed. Push yourself!

exercises.name: The Countdown
exercises.instruction: 5 desk pushups + 5 lunges each leg. Then 4 and 4, 3 and 3, down to 1 and 1. No rest between sets. Time yourself!

exercises.name: Myo-Rep Walking Lunges
exercises.instruction: 15 walking lunges per leg. Take 3-5 deep breaths. Do 3-5 more per leg. Repeat for 5 total sets.

exercises.name: Skater Jumps
exercises.instruction: Jump laterally from one foot to the other, like a speed skater. 15 seconds. Great for balance and cardio.

quotes: It is NEVER too late to start.
Your body does NOT need weights to change; it requires resistance.
The fun part is the journey and watching yourself get better.
More reps. That's the secret to success.
No matter how out of shape, you can do this.
A little movement now adds up to a big change.
You don't need a gym. You just need to start.
Come on, you can do this! Let's go!
The last three or four reps is what makes the muscle grow.
Strength does not come from winning.
The worst thing you can do is nothing.
Just remember, you can't climb the ladder of success with your hands in your pockets.
